name: Greek entity oceanus
shortDescription: Oceanus is the Titan god of the great, earth-encircling river Oceanus, from which all other bodies of water were believed to spring. He is a primordial deity, representing the freshwater source of the world.
description: Oceanus is the Titan god of the great, earth-encircling river or stream of Oceanus in Greek mythology. He was believed to be the source of all the world's freshwater – rivers, springs, and seas. As a Titan, Oceanus was a son of Uranus (Sky) and Gaia (Earth), and thus part of the older generation of gods predating the Olympians. He is typically depicted as an elderly man with a long beard, often with crab claws adorning his head and a serpentine lower body. Unlike most of his Titan brethren, Oceanus did not participate in the Titanomachy, the great war between the Titans and the Olympians, choosing instead to remain neutral. This neutrality may have contributed to his continued status and respect even after the Olympian victory. He is married to his sister, Tethys, and together they are the parents of numerous Oceanids (sea nymphs) and the Potamoi (river gods). While his influence waned as Poseidon's power grew, Oceanus retained a significant role as a fundamental aspect of the cosmos and a personification of the primordial waters surrounding the earth. His domain was seen as the origin and boundary of the world. He is also associated with the East, where the sun was thought to rise from his waters.
